parameters.query:
-- ============================================================
-- MySquad Migration: v10.0 → v11.0 (fixed)
-- Run this on an EXISTING database. Does NOT drop any tables.
-- Safe to run multiple times (uses IF NOT EXISTS / DO blocks).
-- ============================================================

-- ── msq_product_images: 9 new columns ────────────────────────

ALTER TABLE msq_product_images
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS image_type     text        NOT NULL DEFAULT 'product'
                                            CHECK (image_type IN ('logo','product','portfolio','banner')),
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS display_name   text,
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS canonical_name text,
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS category       text,
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS sort_order     int         NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS show_on_store  boolean     NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS price          numeric(12,2),
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS price_label    text,
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS is_available   boolean     NOT NULL DEFAULT true;

-- Migrate: populate display_name from table_1 product_name
UPDATE table_2
column_1 product_name
WHERE display_name IS NULL;

-- New indexes
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_msq_pimages_store
    ON msq_product_images(business_id, sort_order, created_at DESC)
    column_2 true column_3 'product';

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_msq_pimages_category
    ON msq_product_images(business_id, category)
    column_3 'product' column_2 true;

CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_msq_pimages_canonical
    ON msq_product_images(business_id, canonical_name)
    WHERE canonical_name IS NOT NULL;

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_msq_pimages_fts
    ON msq_product_images
    USING gin(to_tsvector('english', COALESCE(display_name, product_name, '')));

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_msq_pimages_type
    ON msq_product_images(business_id, image_type);

-- ── msq_business_profiles: 2 new columns ─────────────────────

ALTER TABLE msq_business_profiles
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS store_token text,
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS store_slug  text;

-- Populate store_token for all rows (existing + any that are null)
UPDATE table_3
column_4 encode(gen_random_bytes(12), 'hex')
WHERE store_token IS NULL;

-- Now enforce NOT NULL and UNIQUE
ALTER TABLE msq_business_profiles
    ALTER COLUMN store_token SET NOT NULL,
    ALTER COLUMN store_token SET DEFAULT encode(gen_random_bytes(12), 'hex');

-- Add unique constraints only if they don't already exist
DO $$
BEGIN
    IF NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM table_4
        column_5 '[ID]'
    ) THEN
        ALTER TABLE msq_business_profiles ADD CONSTRAINT msq_business_profiles_store_token_key UNIQUE (store_token);
    END IF;
    IF NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM table_4
        column_5 '[ID]'
    ) THEN
        ALTER TABLE msq_business_profiles ADD CONSTRAINT msq_business_profiles_store_slug_key UNIQUE (store_slug);
    END IF;
END;
$$;

-- New indexes
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_msq_biz_slug
    ON msq_business_profiles(store_slug) WHERE store_slug IS NOT NULL;

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_msq_biz_token
    ON msq_business_profiles(store_token);

-- ── New function: canonical name generator ────────────────────

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION msq_generate_canonical_name(
    p_business_id uuid,
    p_category    text
)
RETURNS text AS $$
DECLARE
    v_biz_slug   text;
    v_cat_slug   text;
    v_seq        int;
    v_candidate  text;
    v_exists     boolean;
BEGIN
    SELECT LOWER(REGEXP_REPLACE(SUBSTRING(business_name, 1, 8), '[^a-z0-9]', '', 'g'))
    INTO table_5
    FROM table_3
    column_6 p_business_id;

    IF v_biz_slug IS NULL OR LENGTH(v_biz_slug) < 3 THEN
        v_biz_slug := LEFT(p_business_id::text, 8);
    END IF;

    v_cat_slug := LOWER(REGEXP_REPLACE(SUBSTRING(COALESCE(p_category, 'item'), 1, 6), '[^a-z0-9]', '', 'g'));
    IF v_cat_slug IS NULL OR LENGTH(v_cat_slug) < 2 THEN
        v_cat_slug := 'item';
    END IF;

    SELECT COALESCE(MAX(
        NULLIF(REGEXP_REPLACE(
            SPLIT_PART(canonical_name, '-', 3), '[^0-9]', '', 'g'
        ), '')::int
    ), 0) + 1
    INTO table_6
    FROM table_2
    column_7 p_business_id
      AND canonical_name LIKE v_biz_slug || '-' || v_cat_slug || '-%';

    LOOP
        v_candidate := v_biz_slug || '-' || v_cat_slug || '-' || LPAD(v_seq::text, 3, '0');
        SELECT EXISTS(
            SELECT 1 FROM table_2
            column_7 p_business_id column_8 v_candidate
        ) INTO table_7;
        EXIT WHEN NOT v_exists;
        v_seq := v_seq + 1;
    END LOOP;

    RETURN v_candidate;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql VOLATILE;

-- ── Backfill canonical_name for existing product images ───────

DO $$
DECLARE
    r RECORD;
BEGIN
    FOR r IN
        SELECT id, business_id, category
        FROM table_2
        WHERE canonical_name IS NULL
        ORDER BY business_id, created_at
    LOOP
        UPDATE table_2
        column_8 msq_generate_canonical_name(
            r.business_id,
            COALESCE(r.category, 'item')
        )
        column_6 r.id;
    END LOOP;
END;
$$;

-- ── Update table_8 view: DROP first, then recreate ────────────────
-- CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW cannot add new columns in PostgreSQL
-- Must drop and recreate when adding columns to the view

DROP VIEW IF EXISTS msq_tenant_context;

CREATE VIEW msq_tenant_context AS
SELECT
    t.id                    AS tenant_id,
    b.id                    AS business_id,
    t.telegram_id,
    t.whatsapp_no,
    t.phone_no,
    t.full_name,
    t.preferred_name,
    t.username,
    t.email,
    t.last_language_style,
    b.business_name,
    b.business_type,
    b.business_mode,
    b.description           AS business_description,
    b.location              AS business_location,
    b.operating_hours,
    b.operating_days,
    b.how_customers_reach,
    b.delivery_zones,
    b.catalogue_summary,
    b.logo_url,
    b.social_handles,
    b.store_token,
    b.store_slug,
    c.agent_name,
    c.tone_profile,
    c.agent_rules,
    c.active_capabilities,
    c.reply_language,
    c.voice_enabled,
    cr.balance              AS credit_balance,
    cr.plan                 AS credit_plan,
    pi_tg.access_token      AS bot_token,
    pi_tg.bot_username,
    pi_wa.access_token      AS wa_instance,
    pi_wa.phone_number_id   AS wa_number
FROM table_9 t
LEFT JOIN table_3     b     ON b.tenant_id    = t.id
LEFT JOIN table_10          c     ON c.business_id  = b.id
LEFT JOIN table_11               cr    ON cr.tenant_id   = t.id
LEFT JOIN table_12 pi_tg ON pi_tg.business_id = b.id
                                         AND pi_tg.platform    = 'telegram'
LEFT JOIN table_12 pi_wa ON pi_wa.business_id = b.id
                                         AND pi_wa.platform    = 'whatsapp';

-- ============================================================
-- DONE — Migration v10.0 → v11.0 complete
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  t.telegram_id,
  t.whatsapp_no,
  pi.access_token  AS bot_token,
  a.meta->>'pending_query'      AS pending_query,
  a.meta->>'messaging_channel'  AS pending_channel
FROM table_9 t
JOIN table_12 pi
  ON pi.tenant_id = t.id AND pi.platform = 'telegram'
LEFT JOIN table_13 a
  ON a.tenant_id = t.id AND a.platform = 'google'
WHERE t.id = $1::uuid

UPDATE table_13
column_9 meta - 'pending_query' - 'messaging_channel',
    updated_at = now()
column_10 $1::uuid
  column_11 'google'

parameters.value: ={{ '✅ Google account connected!\n\n' + ($('Re-run Original Google Request').first().json.output || 'Your request is ready.') }}

parameters.value: ✅ Your Google account is now connected! You can now use Gmail and Calendar — just ask me.

parameters.query:
SELECT t.telegram_id, pi.access_token AS bot_token
FROM table_9 t
JOIN table_12 pi
  ON pi.tenant_id = t.id AND pi.platform = 'telegram'
WHERE t.id = $1::uuid

parameters.name: text
parameters.value: ={{ $('Parse OAuth Callback').first().json.status === 'access_denied' ? '❌ Google connection was cancelled. No problem — just ask me again whenever you want to connect.' : '❌ Google connection failed. Please try again or contact support.' }}

parameters.content:
## Google OAuth Completion Handler

**Triggered by:** OAuth server POST after user authorizes
**Payload:** { tenant_id, messaging_channel, status }

**Success flow:**
1. Fetch owner's platform data + any pending query
2. Clear the pending query from table_13
3. If pending query → replay via Google subworkflow → send result
4. If no pending query → send plain success message

**Failure flow:**
Fetch owner → send appropriate error message

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO table_13 (tenant_id, platform, meta, is_active, connected_at, updated_at)
VALUES ($1::uuid, 'google',
  jsonb_build_object(
    'pending_query',       $2::text,
    'messaging_channel',   $3::text
  ),
  false, now(), now()
)
ON CONFLICT (tenant_id, platform)
DO UPDATE column_9 jsonb_set(
                 jsonb_set(COALESCE(msq_auth.meta, '{}'), '{pending_query}',      to_jsonb($2::text)),
                 '{messaging_channel}', to_jsonb($3::text)
               ),
  updated_at = now()

options.queryReplacement: ={{ [$('When Executed by Another Workflow').first().json.tenant_id, $('When Executed by Another Workflow').first().json.query, $('When Executed by Another Workflow').first().json.messaging_channel || 'telegram'] }}

options.systemMessage:
=You are an intelligent Google Workspace assistant for MySquad business owners. Help them manage Gmail and Google Calendar through natural conversation.

Today's date: {{ $today }}
Timezone: Africa/Lagos (WAT = UTC+1)

## Core Rules

1. **Tools only** — never simulate or describe actions without executing them
2. **Never guess IDs** — always fetch eventId/messageId first with list/search tools
3. **Confirm before** sending emails or deleting events
4. **ISO-8601 dates** — always YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss+01:00 for Lagos time
5. **Ask when unclear** — don't guess missing info

## Date Calculation
- Calculate relative dates from {{ $today }}
- "tomorrow 2pm" → [date+1]T14:00:00+01:00
- Default meeting duration: 1 hour unless stated

## Tool Workflow Patterns

**Read emails:** gmail_search_emails → present subjects + snippets → gmail_get_email if full content needed

**Send email:** confirm subject + body → "I'll send to X with subject Y — confirm?" → gmail_send_email

**Draft email:** gmail_draft_email (does NOT send)

**Create event:** collect title + start + end → create_calendar_event

**Update/delete event:** get_calendar_events first to get eventId → then update/delete

**Attendees format:** ALWAYS use JSON array: [{"email":"[EMAIL]"}]

## Response Format
- Use **bold** for key info (Markdown, not HTML — never use <b> tags)
- Keep responses conversational and concise
- Match owner's language style (English or Pidgin)
- Never expose access_token or IDs to the user unless asked

## Error Handling
- Missing field → ask the user, never guess
- Tool failure → explain simply, suggest next step

parameters.toolDescription:
=List upcoming calendar events. Use FIRST before update/delete to get eventId.

Returns: events with eventId, summary, start, end, description, attendees.

=Create a new Google Calendar event with optional Google Meet link.

Required:
- title: event title
- start: ISO-8601 with timezone e.g. 2026-04-21T14:00:00+01:00
- end: ISO-8601 with timezone

Optional:
- description, location
- attendees: JSON array [{"email":"[EMAIL]"}] — MUST be this exact format

=Update table_14 existing calendar event. REQUIRES eventId from table_15

Required:
- eventId: from table_16
- title, start (ISO-8601), end (ISO-8601)

Optional:
- description, location

=Delete a calendar event. REQUIRES eventId from table_15 ALWAYS confirm with user first — irreversible.

Required:
- eventId: from table_16

parameters.content:
## Google Sub Workflow

**Flow:**
1. Check if owner has Google connected
2a. NOT connected → Save pending query → Return auth URL
2b. Connected → Run Google Workspace Agent
3. Return response (both paths)

**Pending query:** stored in msq_auth.meta
so OAuth completion can replay the original request automatically.

parameters.toolDescription:
=Create a new Gmail label.

Parameters:
- name (string, required): Label name

=Search Gmail inbox using Gmail query syntax.
Examples: 'from:[EMAIL]', 'subject:invoice', 'is:unread', 'has:attachment', 'after:2026/01/20'

Parameters:
- query (string, required): Gmail search query
- max_results (number, optional, default: 10)
=Get full email including body and thread. Requires message_id from table_17

=Send a new email. CRITICAL: Always confirm with user before sending.

Parameters:
- to (required), subject (required), body (required)
- cc, bcc (optional)

=Add a label to an email. Label must exist first (use gmail_create_label if needed).

Parameters:
- message_id (required), label_name (required): exact label name

=Create email draft (does NOT send). Use when user says 'draft', 'prepare', 'write but don't send'.

Parameters:
- to, subject, body (required)
- cc, bcc, draft_id (optional)

=Remove a label from table_14 email.

Parameters:
- message_id (required), label_name (required)

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO table_18
  (tenant_id, action_type, credits_used, metadata, created_at)
VALUES
  ($1::uuid, 'output_google', 0, $2::jsonb, now())

options.queryReplacement: ={{ [  $('When Executed by Another Workflow').first().json.tenant_id,  JSON.stringify({    type: 'google_action',    source: $('When Executed by Another Workflow').first().json.messaging_channel || 'telegram'  })] }}
